Prompt
U
Oracle Events
- Search or list Airtable relocation records (via airtable_list_records with base_id "appMoveOpsOps001" and table_name "tblRelocations01") to retrieve all active and completed relocations for each coordinator - Fatimah, Blessing, Javier, and Suki - and identify status discrepancies, missing documentation, and records that are wrong or out of date.
- Search or list Linear issues (via linear_list_issues with team "Operations" or similar) to pull open and closed tickets and cross-reference ticket states against Airtable record statuses, surfacing the Raj Patel "Done" ticket vs. "In Progress" record split and DOT placarding documentation gaps.
- Search QuickBooks bills (via quickbooks_search_bills with criteria filtering on vendor "Heartland" or similar, or quickbooks_show_data) to find bill HM-2026-Q1-0042 and verify the $12,800 billed amount, the 3 disputed moves totaling $4,800, and the payment hold status for vendor analysis.
- Search CRM for account managers (via crm_search_deals with query "BrightLoop" or similar, crm_search_companies with query "NorthWind" or similar, crm_list_engagements, or crm_show_data) to identify which account managers handle BrightLoop, NorthWind, Canopy, GreenStack, and Mosaic - the company names are not given in the prompt and must be discovered through investigation.
- Look up email addresses for account managers and sender (via contacts_search_contacts with query "Mina Hashimoto," "Catalina Dubois," "Emeka Diallo," and "Chloe Vance" or similar) to retrieve mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, catalina.dubois@moveops.com, emeka.diallo@moveops.com for the heads-up emails and chloe.vance@moveops.com as the required sender parameter. The agent may also discover these addresses from email threads or CRM data.
- Search Slack for coordinator incidents and vendor discussions (via conversations_search_messages with queries such as "Blessing," "Heartland," "Swift," "piano," "placarding" or similar, and/or conversations_history on relevant channels) to find Blessing's Emilia Cruz piano damage admission, Blessing's DOT placarding gap, the Heartland billing thread, Swift rate increase discussions, and coordinator capacity context.
- Search emails for vendor correspondence and client updates (via search_emails with queries such as "Road Runner," "Marcus Webb," "Heartland," "Swift," "BrightLoop," "Lakeline" or similar) to find the Road Runner delay notification, Marcus Webb follow-ups, Heartland dispute correspondence, Swift rate change notices, BrightLoop expansion details, Dr. Mehta engagement details, and Lakeline/Sandra Walsh outreach.
- Update Airtable record for Lily Marchetti (via airtable_update_records with base_id "appMoveOpsOps001," table_id "tblRelocations01," record ID "recReloc00000002") to change Status from "Completed" to "Cancelled" with a note referencing the Feb 10 cancellation and Heartland billing dispute.
- Update Airtable record for Danielle Osei (via airtable_update_records with base_id "appMoveOpsOps001," table_id "tblRelocations01," record ID "recReloc00000004") to change Status from "Completed" to "Cancelled" with a note referencing the Feb 13 cancellation and Heartland billing dispute.
- Update Airtable record for Raj Patel (via airtable_update_records with base_id "appMoveOpsOps001," table_id "tblRelocations01," record ID "recRajPatelChicagoDenver") to change Status from "In Progress" to "Completed," correct the move dates from April 21-23 to the actual move date of April 10, and add incident documentation about the chain-of-custody failure.
- Create a Linear issue (via linear_create_issue with team "Operations" or similar) for tracking May coordinator assignments, with description covering the BrightLoop expansion batch, current coordinator loads, vendor constraints, and pending items.
- Post the full rundown to Slack #operations (via conversations_add_message with channel_id "C006") with a summary of the coordinator audit, vendor status, May move assignments, and record corrections.
- Send an email (via send_email) from chloe.vance@moveops.com to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, flagging BrightLoop vendor risk - Oliver Bancroft with no vendor coverage, Jonathan Marsh with no pricing tier, Swift surcharge impact on BrightLoop moves, and the Dr. Mehta $12,170 estimate concern.
- Send an email (via send_email) from chloe.vance@moveops.com to catalina.dubois@moveops.com, flagging the Raj Patel chain-of-custody incident for NorthWind, and the Marchetti/Osei record corrections and Heartland dispute impact for Canopy.
- Send an email (via send_email) from chloe.vance@moveops.com to emeka.diallo@moveops.com, flagging the Zara Okoye prototype damage incident for Mosaic Robotics and hazmat/vendor issues for GreenStack Energy.
- Report a comprehensive coordinator audit summary to the user (Chloe) covering all four coordinators - Fatimah, Blessing, Javier, and Suki - including workload counts, specific incidents, record-vs-reality gaps, and documentation issues.
- Report the vendor analysis for May to the user (Chloe), covering the Heartland dispute details, Swift rate increase, Lakeline backup limitations, BrightLoop May move assignments with vendor needs and coverage gaps, Dr. Mehta executive relocation, and any moves where vendor problems could push the timeline.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
